chapter_title.ar: باب ضَمَانِ الْجَمَّالِ وَ الْمُكَارِي وَ أَصْحَابِ السُّفُنِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 113 – The liability of the cameleer, and the hirer (of animals), and the owners of the ships

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/301)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/301)
text: الْجَمَّالِ يَكْسِرُ الَّذِي يَحْمِلُ أَوْ يُهَرِيقُهُ قَالَ إِنْ كَانَ مَأْمُوناً فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْ‏ءٌ وَ إِنْ كَانَ غَيْرَ مَأْمُونٍ فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Abbas Bin Musa, from Yunus, from Ibn Muskan, from Abu Baseer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the cameleer who breaks that which he carries, or spills it. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If he was trustworthy, so there is nothing upon him, but if he was without trustworthiness, so he is liable’.
en.sarwar: 6. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-‘Abbas ibn Musa from Yunus from ibn Muskan from abu Basir who has said the following: “About the case of a camel man who breaks what he carries or spills, abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al- Salam, has said, ‘If he is trusted, there is nothing on him; but if he is not trusted then he is liable.’”
